question: Calculate the variance and standard deviation of the following sample: $2,3,3,3,4$.
answer:
If you calculate the values of $s$ and $s^2$ using the formula in the box on the previous page, you first need to compute $\bar{x}$. From the table below, we see that $\Sigma x=15$. Thus, $\bar{x}=\frac{\sum x}{n}=\frac{15}{5}=3$. Now, for each measurement, find $(x-\bar{x})$ and $(x-\bar{x})^2$, as shown.

\begin{tabular}{ccc}
\hline$x$ & $(x-\bar{x})$ & $(x-\bar{x})^2$ \\
\hline 2 & -1 & 1 \\
3 & 0 & 0 \\
3 & 0 & 0 \\
3 & 0 & 0 \\
4 & 1 & 1 \\
\hline$\Sigma x=15$ & & $\Sigma(x-\bar{x})^2=2$ \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Then we use ${ }^{\dagger}$

$$
\begin{aligned}
s^2 & =\frac{\sum_{i=1}^n(x-\bar{x})^2}{n-1}=\frac{2}{5-1}=\frac{2}{4}=.5 \\
s & =\sqrt{.5}=.71
\end{aligned}
$$

meta_info.caption: Computing Measures of Variation
meta_info.chapter: Methods for Describing Sets of Data
